prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (v7 step 21) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in EVERY structural element — same dual-state composition, same hand poses, same vial appearance (transparent tube + red blood + plain ribbed cap), same tube proportions, same arrow, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight, same outlined line-art letter treatment.

>>> THE DIALED TEXT ON THE BLACK CAP IS WRONG. FLIP IT 180°. <<<

The text on the BLACK CAP of the protector tube in the RIGHT state currently reads in the WRONG DIRECTION top-down. ROTATE the entire DIALED text band 180° in place (around its center) so the letters are swapped end-for-end:
  • Whatever letter was at the TOP of the cap moves to the BOTTOM.
  • Whatever letter was at the BOTTOM of the cap moves to the TOP.
  • Each individual letter is also rotated 180° so it remains right-side-up after the band flip.
  • RESULT: reading the cap top-down must spell D, I, A, L, E, D = 'DIALED' forward. The FIRST D sits at the very top of the cap. The LAST D sits at the bottom of the cap just above the cap/body seam.

The HEALTH text on the WHITE BODY (both LEFT and RIGHT panels) is ALSO wrong. APPLY THE SAME 180° FLIP to it:
  • Whatever letter was at the TOP of the body moves to the BOTTOM.
  • Whatever letter was at the BOTTOM of the body moves to the TOP.
  • Each individual letter rotated 180° to remain right-side-up.
  • RESULT: reading the body top-down must spell H, E, A, L, T, H = 'HEALTH' forward. The FIRST H sits at the top of the body. The LAST H sits at the bottom of the body.

The body shows ONLY 'HEALTH'. No 'DIALED' on the body.

When this fix is correct, reading top-down on the assembled tube (RIGHT panel): D-I-A-L-E-D-H-E-A-L-T-H linearly. The two words together spell 'DIALED HEALTH' top-down.

The SECOND reference image is the approved step 17 which has the protector tube text rendered in the CORRECT orientation. The text on your output's tube must look like the text on step 17's tube. If you replicate the text orientation from the FIRST reference (v7), the output is WRONG. You must REVERSE the text band on both sections from how the FIRST reference shows them.

Do NOT modify anything else — same hands, same vial, same blood, same ribbed vial cap, same tube proportions, same arrow, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic. The ONLY change is the 180° flip of both text bands on the tube.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers.
